pattern: Find all occurrences of pattern a in s, then for each such index check if any occurrence of b is within distance k using a sorted list and binary search.
hints: First collect all starting indices where pattern a occurs in s, and all starting indices where pattern b occurs in s.
For each candidate index i (where a occurs), use binary search on the sorted list of b-occurrence indices to find the nearest one — if its distance from i is <= k, index i is beautiful.
Python's string find or KMP can enumerate the occurrences; the result must be returned in sorted order.

edge_inputs.description: a not found in s — empty result
b not found in s — no beautiful index
a equals b — every occurrence is its own nearby match
k is just barely enough to bridge two occurrences

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    chars = string.ascii_lowercase[:6]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(size))
    a = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 4)))
    b = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 4)))
    k = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [s, a, b, k]
